en.extract:
The song parrot or singing parrot (Geoffroyus heteroclitus) is a species of parrot in the family Psittaculidae.


Overview
Song parrot is found in the Bismarck Archipelago and on Bougainville Island in Papua New Guinea, and in the Solomon Islands except Rennel. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. This species demonstrates a large to small landmass inter-island colonization pattern based on its presence in Molehanua village, Ugi Island.

The '''song parrot''' or '''singing parrot''' ('''''Geoffroyus heteroclitus''''') is a species of [[parrot]] in the family [[Psittaculidae]].

==Overview==
Song parrot is found in the [[Bismarck Archipelago]] and on [[Bougainville Island]] in [[Papua New Guinea]], and in the [[Solomon Islands]] except [[Rennel]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is [[subtropical]] or [[tropical]] moist lowland [[forest]]s. This species demonstrates a large to small landmass inter-island colonization pattern based on its presence in Molehanua village, Ugi Island.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Sardell |first=Jason M. |date=April 2016 |title=Recent Dispersal Events among Solomon Islands Bird Species Reveal Differing Potential Routes of Island Colonization |url=http://www.bioone.org/doi/10.2984/70.2.6 |journal=Pacific Science |language=en |volume=70 |issue=2 |pages=201–208 |doi=10.2984/70.2.6 |issn=0030-8870|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
